返回本批次

人工智能营销 BS 指数

The AI Marketing BS Index

bastian.rieck.me 作者 speckx 分数 95 评论 19 2026/04/02 01:55
打开 HN 原帖 打开原始文章

中文摘要

  1. 你可能已经知道约翰·贝兹的“疯狂指数”，它提供了一个简单的半开玩笑的评分系统，用于评估对物理学的研究贡献是否可能是“革命性的”。该清单包含诸如“将自己与伽利略进行比较的 40 分，表明现代宗教裁判所正在努力处理你的案件，等等”之类的宝石。在大型语言模型的时代，我们迫切需要类似的东西，但用于“营销语言”。感觉就像每家公司想要真正推销一款可能为世界做点好事的有趣产品时，至少还有另外三个公司将“氛围检查”调到了十一项，并滥用了他们能得到的任何术语。因此，这是对营销材料和评分标准进行分类的一次卑微尝试。
  2. 你的概念以-5分开始。让我们相信每个人都是无罪的。在没有提供任何引文、论文或其他说明的情况下“发明”某物，得 10 分。数学、物理或生命科学中的术语按理不应出现，每个实例得 10 分。 20 分，用于以“这不是 X，这是 Y”的形式进行通常的“motte-and bailey”或对冲。以伪深奥的废话结尾的段落扣20分。声称该产品具有“自然”或“宇宙”的功能，得 20 分。 20 分，因为在显然没有保证的情况下提及“新兴属性”。每次放弃常春藤盟校名誉，扣 20 分。 “技术”描述中没有任何可证伪的主张或预测，得 30 分。每一项无法验证的研究“合作”得 40 分。
  3. 随着时间的推移，我确信我必须想出一个更长的得分。在那之前，愿您的收件箱里没有空无内容的长电子邮件。

Original Summary

  1. You may already be aware of John Baez’s “Crackpot Index”, which provides a simple tongue-in-cheek scoring system for assessing whether a research contribution to physics is likely “revolutionary.” The list contain such gems as “40 points for comparing yourself to Galileo, suggesting that a modern-day Inquisition is hard at work on your case, and so on.” In the age of large language models, we direly need something similar but for “marketing-speak.” It feels like for every company that wants to actually pitch an interesting product that might do something good for the world, there are at least three others that turned up the “vibe check” to eleven and misuse any bit of terminology they can get their hands on. Thus, a humble attempt at classifying marketing materials along with a grading rubric.
  2. Your concept starts with -5 points. Let’s give everyone the benefit of the doubt. 10 points for “inventing” something without providing any citation, paper, or other specification. 10 points for each instance of a term from mathematics, physics, or the life sciences where, by rights, it should not appear. 20 points for doing the usual motte-and-bailey or hedging in the form of “It is not X. It is Y.” 20 points for ending a paragraph with pseudo-profound nonsense. 20 points for claiming that the product does what “nature” does or what “the universe” does. 20 points for referring to “emergent properties” where this is clearly not warranted. 20 points for each instance of dropping Ivy League namedropping. 30 points for having no falsifiable claims or predictions anywhere in the “technical” description. 40 points for each research “collaboration” that cannot be verified.
  3. As time progresses, I am sure I will have to come up with an extended scoring. Until then, may your inbox be blissfully empty of long e-mails devoid of content.

评论（默认中文）

n1tro_lab 2026/04/02 05:19
如果您的“AI 代理”是一个 ChatGPT 包装器，可以读取 CSV 并发送 Slack 消息，但您的推介材料上写着“自主多代理编排平台”，则为 500 分
500 points if your "AI agent" is a ChatGPT wrapper that reads a CSV and sends a Slack message but your pitch deck says "autonomous multi-agent orchestration platform"
在 HN 中查看
convexly 2026/04/02 05:57
如果您的“专有模型”是带有系统提示的 GPT-4，并且您称其为您的“研究实验室”，则获得 1000 分
1000 points if your "proprietary model" is GPT-4 with a system prompt and you call it your "research lab"
在 HN 中查看
postsantum 2026/04/02 06:21
研究就是科学，科学不在乎你相信什么！
Research is Science and Science doens't care what you believe!
在 HN 中查看
swyx 2026/04/02 03:07
- 报告基准，方便地省略众所周知的 SOTA，20 分

- 方便地忽略众所周知的基准，因为不是 SOTA，30 分

- 更改 GRPO 中的一个小术语并将其命名为完全不同的缩写，50 分

- 尝试滑入系统黑客，但将你的论文命名为模型改进，100 分（奖励给第一个找出我在这里转推哪篇最新论文的回复者）

- 福布斯 30 位 30 岁以下精英，100 分

- report benchmark that conveniently omits well known SOTAs, 20 points

- conveniently omit well known benchmarks because not SOTA, 30 points

- change one tiny term in GRPO and call it a completely different acronym, 50 points

- try to slide in a systems hack and but title your paper as though it is a model improvement, 100 points (prizes to the first replier who figures out which recent paper i am subtweeting here)

- forbes 30 under 30, 100 points

too far, at this point you go to jail

fine tune an oss model and call it a groundbreaking innovation -- 20 points
在 HN 中查看
argee 2026/04/02 09:07
作者甚至链接到莫特和贝利谬误（学说？）[0]，但读完后我发现与“这不是 X，这是 Y”没有任何关系？

[0] https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Motte-and-bailey_fallacy

The author has gone so far as to link to Motte-and-Bailey fallacy (doctrine?) [0], but after reading it I see no relation to "It's not X, it's Y"?

[0] https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Motte-and-bailey_fallacy

在 HN 中查看
justonceokay 2026/04/02 10:46
仁慈地我认为事情会是这样的：

答：我们已经开发了下一代人工智能。

B：所以这个模型/产品采取了更多类似人类的动作，或者有一个更完全实现的世界模型？

答：人工智能并不是大型语言模型的同义词。我们刚刚制作了一个 LLM，它使用的 RAM 减少了 10%。

在这种情况下，简短的版本将是“这不是一般智力，而是法学硕士”。它之所以是莫特和贝利，是因为你有一个 100 张幻灯片，这意味着你的产品可以写出下一部伟大的美国小说——暗示贝利存在。

Charitably I think it would go like this:

A: We’ve developed the next generation of Artificial Intelligence.

B: So this model/product takes more human-like actions or has a more fully-realized world model?

A: no AI is a term of art synonymous to large language models. We just made an LLM that uses 10% less RAM.

In this case the short version would be “it’s not general intelligence, it’s an LLM”. The reason it’s a motte and bailey is because you have a 100 slide deck implying your product can write the next great American novel—implying the bailey exists.

-10 points if you actually read your marketing yourself before copy and pasting it on the website

I submit that doing (4) earns 40 points, rather than 20.

在 HN 中查看
heresie-dabord 2026/04/02 07:20
启发 TFA 的 Crackpot Index [1] 的要点：

每一项被广泛认为是错误的陈述得 1 分。

每一条明显空洞的陈述得 2 分。

每一条逻辑上不一致的陈述扣 3 分。

对于尽管仔细更正但仍然遵守的每项此类陈述，得 5 分。

使用与广泛接受的真实实验结果相矛盾的思想实验，得 5 分。

每个全大写字母的单词得 5 分（键盘有缺陷的除外）。

指出你已经上过学，就得 10 分，就好像这是神智正常的证据一样。

对于您发明和使用但没有正确定义的每个新术语，扣 10 分。

10 分用于论证当前已确立的理论“只是一个理论”，就好像这在某种程度上是反对它的观点。

暗示你应该获得诺贝尔奖 20 分。

通过提出（真实的或想象的）对你过去的理论的嘲笑来为自己辩护，得 20 分。

以自己的名字命名某物可得 20 分。 （例如，当你的名字恰好是埃文斯时，谈论“埃文斯场方程”。）

20 分用于谈论你的理论有多么伟大，但从未真正解释它。

将那些反对你的想法的人比作纳粹、冲锋队或褐衫军，得 40 分。

[1] _ https://math.ucr.edu/home/baez/crackpot.html

Highlights from the Crackpot Index [1] that inspired TFA:

1 point for every statement that is widely agreed on to be false.

2 points for every statement that is clearly vacuous.

3 points for every statement that is logically inconsistent.

5 points for each such statement that is adhered to despite careful correction.

5 points for using a thought experiment that contradicts the results of a widely accepted real experiment.

5 points for each word in all capital letters (except for those with defective keyboards).

10 points for pointing out that you have gone to school, as if this were evidence of sanity.

10 points for each new term you invent and use without properly defining it.

10 points for arguing that a current well-established theory is "only a theory", as if this were somehow a point against it.

20 points for suggesting that you deserve a Nobel prize.

20 points for defending yourself by bringing up (real or imagined) ridicule accorded to your past theories.

20 points for naming something after yourself. (E.g., talking about the "The Evans Field Equation" when your name happens to be Evans.)

20 points for talking about how great your theory is, but never actually explaining it.

40 points for comparing those who argue against your ideas to Nazis, stormtroopers, or brownshirts.

[1] _ https://math.ucr.edu/home/baez/crackpot.html

在 HN 中查看
PaulHoule 2026/04/02 03:34
该清单避免了许多真正的罪过，并且有很多错误分析，例如，

   20 points for doing the usual motte-and-bailey or hedging in the form of “It is not X. It is Y.”
我的意思是，这种语言模式通常是合适的，但对于今天关注的人来说，它是……某些东西的标志。

我的意思是，我厌倦了副驾驶给出“你不是皮毛，你是兽人”之类的答案，我认为，对于那些开着灯却没人在家的人来说，这确实是一个示踪剂，就像他们想成为有关人工智能的顶级博主，但他们没有意识到“这不是 X，这是 Y”模式是一种告诉。

The list avoids many of the real sins and has plenty of mis-analysis, for instance,

   20 points for doing the usual motte-and-bailey or hedging in the form of “It is not X. It is Y.”
I mean, that language pattern is often appropriate but for people who are paying attention today it is a sign of... something.

I mean, I am tired of Copilot giving answers like "You're not a fur, you're a therianthrope" It is really a tracer, I think, for someone for whom the lights are on and nobody is home, like they want to be a top blogger about AI but they haven't caught on that the "It's not X, it's Y" pattern is a tell.

在 HN 中查看
